The Problem

Classic vs. In-Situ

Measuring moisture only at intervals means losing control over the entire process.

❌ Classic Measurement
Sample extraction required
Time delay through lab analysis
Single measurements only
Interference with running process
✓ Inline Moisture Control
Measurement directly in material flow
Continuous real-time data
Values immediately available
No interference, no production stop
Advantages

Built for Industry

Robust, precise and seamlessly integrable into existing plants.

⚡
Real-Time

Continuous measurement data without delay – directly usable for monitoring and controlling your process.

🔧
Robust Design

Designed for harsh industrial environments – low-maintenance and reliable under real process conditions.

🔗
Integration

Compatible with 4–20 mA, digital interfaces and PLC systems – easy integration into existing installations.

🎯
Precision

Highly accurate readings directly in the material – without sampling, without distortion from transport losses.

📍
In-Situ Measurement

The device is installed directly in the process – on conveyors, in dryers, silos or production lines.

📉
Cost Efficiency

Avoid over- and under-drying, optimize energy use, reduce rejects – measurably lower operating costs.

How It Works

Simple as that

Three steps from installation to process optimization.

1
Installation

The device is mounted directly in the process – on the conveyor, in the dryer or in the material flow.

2
Measurement

Continuous real-time capture of material moisture – no sampling, no process interruption.

3
Control

Readings feed directly into your plant – for monitoring, regulation and optimization of the entire process.

Economic Value

What it delivers

Continuous moisture measurement pays off directly.

01

Stable Production

Detect and eliminate process fluctuations through seamless monitoring.

02

Consistent Quality

Actively prevent over- and under-drying – for consistently uniform results.

03

Less Energy Use

Control drying processes more precisely and reduce unnecessary energy consumption.

04

Lower Operating Costs

Low-maintenance design and less reject material measurably reduce ongoing costs.

Specifications

Technical Data

Designed for industrial requirements.

Measuring Method In-situ (Microwave / NIR / Capacitive)
Measuring Range On request – configurable per process
Response Time Real-time / continuous (< 1 s)
Temperature Range Suited for industrial applications
Interfaces 4–20 mA · Digital · PLC-compatible
Locations Conveyors · Dryers · Silos · Material Flows
Design Robust · low-maintenance · industrial-grade
Integration Retrofittable into existing installations
